explanation: The LTL property states that globally, if task priorities change, the robot should provide an explanation.  This means at every point in time if the priorities are changed, the robot provides an explanation.
This LTL property states that globally, if the task priorities change, then at every future time step, the robot will provide an explanation about it.
This LTL property states that if task priorities change (bool_exp1) then, the robot will always explain the change (bool_exp3). The explanation itself is not constrained in this template.
This LTL formula states that globally, if task priorities change (bool_exp1), then it is always the case that the system should provide an explanation (bool_exp3).

The LTL property states that if task priorities change, then the robot will explain the changes in its plan for a certain duration. Specifically, the explanation must be provided for the duration N_DURATION, or the changes must be explained until the mission is completed.

This LTL property states that globally, if task priorities are changing, then the robot should explain. This ensures the robot always provides explanations when task priorities shift, fulfilling the prompt's requirement.
The LTL property ensures that if task priorities change, then the robot globally explains (i.e., provide an explanation).

This LTL property ensures that globally, whenever task priorities change, the robot must explain. This is modeled using a G (globally) operator, ensuring that the condition holds true at every time step. The implication is that the 'changePlan' condition triggers the 'explain' action.

The LTL property ensures that whenever task priorities change, the robot must explain the situation and any relevant information. Specifically, the property requires that the robot provides an explanation if task priorities change, the explanation is provided by returning all available alternative solutions.
chosen_LTL_template: ((G ((! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1)))) | (X (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3))))) & ((! bool_exp1) -> (((! bool_exp1) & (X bool_exp1)) V (bool_exp2 -> bool_exp3))))

explanation: This LTL property states that globally, if task priorities change, the robot should provide an explanation. This is captured by the G (Globally) operator ensuring this happens at every time step. The template ID 127 is chosen because it encapsulates the core requirement.

The LTL property states that if task priorities change at any point in time, then the robot must always explain the reason for the change and the explanation must include all of the alternatives.  This is a general requirement for any time step, ensuring the robot always provides an explanation when the task priorities are changed.

This LTL property states that if task priorities change, the robot must always explain why the change in task priorities is occurring. This is captured by the template G(bool_exp1 -> ((G[0,N_DURATION] bool_exp3) | (G bool_exp3))), where bool_exp1 is 'priorities', and bool_exp3 is 'explain'.  N_DURATION determines how long the explanation must be valid for.

The LTL property ensures that whenever task priorities change, the robot will eventually explain the reason behind the change to the plan.  The 'G' (Globally) operator ensures this happens repeatedly. 'bool_exp1' defines the change in plan, and 'bool_exp3' describes the robot providing an explanation.

This LTL property states that if the task priorities change (bool_exp1), then it is always the case that the explanation (bool_exp3) is provided for the changes in task priorities. The property uses the 'G' (Globally) operator to ensure this holds true for all time steps, effectively capturing the requirement that an explanation is given whenever priorities change.
The LTL property ensures that if task priorities change, the robot will always provide an explanation of the plan given those changes.

The LTL property states that globally, if the task priorities are changing (bool_exp1), then the robot provides an explanation (bool_exp3) and it will always be the case that the explanation is provided for a period (N_DURATION) or forever.

The LTL property states that globally, if task priorities change the plan, then the robot will explain. This ensures that the robot provides explanations whenever task priorities lead to a plan change.

This LTL property states that globally, whenever task priorities change, then the robot will explain. This captures the requirement for the robot to provide an explanation whenever task priorities are altered.

The LTL property ensures that if the robot's task priorities change, then globally, the robot should explain its current path, and if the path is changing then the robot should explain its current path for a period.

(bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1))))))))))) & (bool_exp1 -> ((bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1))) V (bool_exp2 -> ((bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1))) | ((X bool_exp3) & (! (bool_exp1 & (X (! bool_exp1))))))))))
